name: Ashwini
description: The Horse Riders - First nakshatra representing healing and swift action
vedicReferences: Mentioned in Taittiriya Brahmana as divine physicians

name: Bharani
description: The Bearer - Nakshatra of birth, death, and transformation
vedicReferences: Connected to Yama in Rig Veda for life-death cycle

name: Krittika
description: The Cutter - Nakshatra of fire, purification, and sharp intellect
vedicReferences: Agni is praised in numerous Rig Vedic hymns as purifier

name: Rohini
description: The Red One - Nakshatra of growth, beauty, and material abundance
vedicReferences: Krishna's birth nakshatra, mentioned in Mahabharata

padas.characteristics: Active growth, pioneering in business, energetic
Maximum material focus, wealth accumulation, Vargottama
Highly emotional, nurturing, family-focused, Moon vargottama

name: Mrigashira
description: The Deer's Head - Nakshatra of search, curiosity, and restlessness
vedicReferences: Associated with Soma's pursuit in Vedic mythology

padas.characteristics: Intense investigation, occult research, Mars vargottama

name: Ardra
description: The Moist One - Nakshatra of storms, transformation, and intense emotions
vedicReferences: Rudra's fierce form mentioned in Shiva Puranas

padas.characteristics: Philosophical intensity, seeking wisdom through storm
Scientific approach, humanitarian destruction of old
Compassionate intensity, spiritual destruction of ego

name: Punarvasu
description: Return of the Light - Nakshatra of renewal, restoration, and good fortune
vedicReferences: Aditi praised in Rig Veda as mother of gods and boundlessness

name: Pushya
description: The Nourisher - Most auspicious nakshatra for growth and prosperity
vedicReferences: Brihaspati is the guru of gods in all Puranas

name: Ashlesha
description: The Entwiner - Nakshatra of serpent energy, kundalini, and mystical power
vedicReferences: Nagas mentioned extensively in Mahabharata and Puranas

name: Magha
description: The Mighty One - Nakshatra of ancestors, royalty, and throne room
vedicReferences: Pitrus extensively mentioned in Garuda Purana

name: Purva Phalguni
description: The Former Reddish One - Nakshatra of pleasure, creativity, and procreation
vedicReferences: Bhaga is mentioned in Rig Veda as god of good fortune

padas.characteristics: Detailed artistic work, service through arts
Maximum artistic expression, relationship focus, Vargottama

name: Uttara Phalguni
description: The Latter Reddish One - Nakshatra of partnership, contracts, and patronage
vedicReferences: Aryaman mentioned in Rig Veda as god of contracts and partnerships

name: Hasta
description: The Hand - Nakshatra of skill, dexterity, and manifestation
vedicReferences: Savitar mentioned in Rig Veda as stimulator and activator

padas.characteristics: Nurturing through craft, emotional skills, Vargottama

name: Chitra
description: The Bright One - Nakshatra of beauty, design, and architecture
vedicReferences: Vishwakarma praised in Rig Veda as divine architect

padas.characteristics: Maximum aesthetic expression, balanced design, Vargottama
Deep aesthetic intensity, transformative design, Mars Vargottama

name: Swati
description: The Independent One - Nakshatra of independence, trade, and wind
symbol: Coral/Sword blade moving in the wind
vedicReferences: Vayu extensively mentioned in Vedas as wind god and prana

padas.characteristics: Humanitarian trade, social reform through business

name: Vishakha
description: The Forked Branch - Nakshatra of goal achievement, determination, and transformation
vedicReferences: Indra-Agni combination mentioned in Rig Veda for power and purification

padas.characteristics: Deep emotional goals, nurturing through achievement

name: Anuradha
description: Following Radha - Nakshatra of friendship, devotion, and balance
vedicReferences: Mitra mentioned in Rig Veda as god of friendship and contracts

padas.characteristics: Intense friendship, deep devotional transformation, Vargottama

name: Jyeshtha
description: The Eldest - Nakshatra of seniority, protection, and occult power
vedicReferences: Indra praised in Rig Veda as king of gods and protector

name: Mula
description: The Root - Nakshatra of investigation, destruction, and foundations
vedicReferences: Nirriti mentioned in Atharva Veda as goddess of destruction and misfortune

padas.characteristics: Material investigation, wealth destruction for renewal

name: Purva Ashadha
description: The Invincible One - Nakshatra of invincibility, purification, and early victory
vedicReferences: Apas mentioned in Rig Veda as purifying water deities

name: Uttara Ashadha
description: The Universal Star - Nakshatra of final victory, permanence, and righteousness
vedicReferences: Vishvadevas mentioned in Vedas as universal gods ensuring cosmic order

name: Shravana
description: The Listener - Nakshatra of learning, listening, and knowledge transmission
vedicReferences: Vishnu's three steps mentioned in Rig Veda as cosmic principle

padas.characteristics: Intuitive learning, nurturing knowledge, Moon Vargottama

name: Dhanishta
description: The Wealthiest - Nakshatra of music, rhythm, and prosperity
vedicReferences: Eight Vasus mentioned in Rig Veda as elemental deities of prosperity

padas.characteristics: Occult music, transformative wealth, Mars Vargottama

name: Shatabhisha
description: The Hundred Healers - Nakshatra of healing, mystery, and solitude
vedicReferences: Varuna mentioned in Rig Veda as god of cosmic order and hidden truth

name: Purva Bhadrapada
description: The Scorching Pair - Nakshatra of transformation, intensity, and spiritual fire
symbol: Front legs of funeral cot/Two-faced man/Sword
vedicReferences: Aja Ekapada mentioned in Vedas as fierce form of Shiva

name: Uttara Bhadrapada
description: The Warrior Star - Nakshatra of depth, wisdom, and kundalini power
vedicReferences: Ahir Budhnya mentioned as serpent of cosmic depths in Vedas

padas.characteristics: Maximum depth, occult wisdom, transformative kundalini

name: Revati
description: The Wealthy - Final nakshatra of nourishment, completion, and safe journey
vedicReferences: Pushan mentioned in Rig Veda as protector of travelers and nourisher

padas.characteristics: Maximum spiritual completion, liberation journey, Vargottama
